note: Small hand-written fixture used to test the local LLM enrichment pipeline. Replace with licensed Reddit/API exports before production use.

comments.text: The cheap stacking cups got more use than almost anything else. Baby chewed them at first, then nested them, then used them in the bath. I like that there are no batteries.
Stacking cups are the one toy I keep recommending. Easy to wash, easy to throw in a diaper bag, and my toddler still uses them for pretend cooking.
They were great until one cracked after being stepped on. I would avoid any set with stickers because the labels peeled after bath use.
We switched from squeeze toys to open bath pipes because I was tired of black mold coming out of rubber ducks. Open pieces are much easier to rinse and inspect.
The suction cups are hit or miss on our tile, but open bath toys are still better than anything sealed. Just separate the pieces and let them dry.
A felt busy board helped on a flight because it was quiet. But check the buckles and little pieces. I would not give it to a toddler who still puts hardware in their mouth.
